applications-software-engineering.claim_text: Agentic systems for software engineering (SWE-agent, SWE-bench, AlphaCode 2, Devin-style code agents) solve real GitHub-issue benchmarks at increasing rates, demonstrating that LLM agents can plan, edit, run, and debug code over long horizons.

candidates.paper_title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?

useful_outcomes.rationale: Introduces SWE-bench, the canonical benchmark of 2,294 real GitHub issues across 12 Python repos; this benchmark is the field's primary measurement instrument for code-agent progress.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Practitioners can run SWE-bench today on any candidate agent to obtain a comparable issue-resolution rate; the dataset and harness are public.

plausible.rationale: Methodology grounds evaluation in held-out PR tests on real repositories — standard and reproducible.

scalable.rationale: Benchmark and Lite/Verified variants scale to large agent fleets and have become the de facto leaderboard for SWE agents.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation strategy (pass real PR test suites) transfers directly to any new agent and is used by hundreds of follow-on systems.

candidates.match_note: Defines the exact benchmark cited in the claim text.

candidates.paper_title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates substantial SWE-bench resolution-rate gains via a purpose-built agent-computer interface, named in the claim text.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source agent and ACI design patterns are directly adoptable; widely forked.

plausible.rationale: Ablations isolate ACI components (file viewer, edit, search) showing why each lifts performance.

scalable.rationale: Scales across SWE-bench instances and HumanEvalFix; long-horizon runs remain bounded by LLM context, but architecture is production-ready.

how_to_validate.rationale: SWE-bench pass@1 and HumanEvalFix provide transferable validation.

candidates.match_note: Directly named in the claim; flagship code-agent paper.

candidates.paper_title: ChatDev: Communicative Agents for Software Development

useful_outcomes.rationale: End-to-end software dev via role-playing agents (CEO, CTO, programmer, tester) producing runnable mini-applications.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source framework usable today for software-generation pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Waterfall-style multi-agent SOP grounded in software-engineering practice.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on small applications; SWE-bench-scale long-horizon repo edits not the focus.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation uses code-quality and runnability metrics rather than held-out issue benchmarks.

candidates.match_note: Code-agent application, but operates on greenfield generation rather than the GitHub-issue paradigm in the claim.

candidates.paper_title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework

useful_outcomes.rationale: Encodes SOPs into multi-agent prompts and reports state-of-the-art on HumanEval/MBPP and a SoftwareDev benchmark.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open framework with role abstractions practitioners can drop into pipelines.

plausible.rationale: SOP-based prompt engineering with documented role contracts mitigates cascading hallucinations.

scalable.rationale: Mid-sized application generation; long-horizon repo evolution not addressed.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validates on HumanEval, MBPP, and SoftwareDev — transferable code-generation benchmarks.

candidates.match_note: Strong code-generation multi-agent system, slight gap from GitHub-issue framing.

candidates.paper_title: Executable Code Actions Elicit Better LLM Agents

useful_outcomes.rationale: CodeAct unifies actions as Python; reports up to 20% absolute gains over JSON/text-action baselines on API-Bank and other agent benchmarks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: CodeAct interpreter and agent are open and adoptable for any tool-using SWE agent.

plausible.rationale: Code-as-action consolidates composition and control flow; well-motivated against JSON-action limits.

scalable.rationale: Python interpreter substrate scales to arbitrary tool sets.

how_to_validate.rationale: API-Bank, MINT, and agent benchmarks provide transferable validation.

candidates.match_note: Underpins many SWE-agent action interfaces; tangential to GitHub-issue resolution rate.

candidates.paper_title: AgentCoder: Multi-Agent-based Code Generation with Iterative Testing and Optimisation

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reports strong pass@1 gains on HumanEval, MBPP, and APPS via programmer/test-designer/executor roles.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Concept is reusable but framework less standardised than ChatDev/MetaGPT.

plausible.rationale: Iterative test-driven optimisation is a sound, well-motivated loop.

scalable.rationale: Evaluated on standard code-gen benchmarks; not yet on repo-scale issues.

how_to_validate.rationale: HumanEval/MBPP/APPS are standard transferable benchmarks.

candidates.match_note: Multi-agent code generation with iterative testing; weak on long-horizon debug task in claim.

candidates.paper_title: Self-Collaboration Code Generation via ChatGPT

useful_outcomes.rationale: Self-collaboration framework (analyst/coder/tester roles) yields large pass@1 improvements on HumanEval/MBPP.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Lightweight prompting recipe immediately reusable on any LLM.

plausible.rationale: Role-based decomposition is well-motivated and reproducible.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on standard code benchmarks rather than full repos.

how_to_validate.rationale: HumanEval, MBPP, BIG-Bench-Hard — transferable code-gen evaluations.

candidates.match_note: Single-LLM self-collaboration; partial overlap with the SWE-agent claim.

candidates.paper_title: GPT-4 Technical Report

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reports strong HumanEval and codex-style coding scores that underpin downstream SWE-agents.

immediate_benefit.rationale: GPT-4 is the default backbone for nearly every code agent in the period.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is well-established; the model is a foundation, not an agent.

scalable.rationale: Production-grade API serving millions of code-agent invocations.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reports HumanEval/exam suites; not focused on agentic SWE validation.

candidates.match_note: Foundation model behind most SWE agents; not itself an agentic SWE paper.

candidates.paper_title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Improves search on Game of 24, creative writing, crosswords; coding-specific results are limited.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Reusable inference pattern but not a SWE-specific recipe.

plausible.rationale: Well-motivated search-over-thoughts methodology.

scalable.rationale: Expensive at scale; rarely used in production SWE agents.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation transfers, but not on SWE benchmarks.

candidates.match_note: Reasoning paradigm relevant to planning in SWE agents but not a software-engineering application.

candidates.paper_title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts

useful_outcomes.rationale: Identifies long-context retrieval failure mode that directly affects long-horizon SWE agents.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Findings inform context-management design for code agents but no agent is released.

plausible.rationale: Careful controlled experiments on multi-doc QA and KV-retrieval.

scalable.rationale: Conclusions hold across multiple long-context LLMs.

how_to_validate.rationale: Position-controlled multi-doc QA protocol transfers to any model.

candidates.match_note: Context-length analysis affecting SWE-agent design; not an applications paper itself.

candidates.paper_title: Large language models encode clinical knowledge

useful_outcomes.rationale: MedQA/Med-PaLM medical QA results — not software engineering.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Adopters are clinicians, not SWE-agent builders.

plausible.rationale: Sound methodology in its own domain.

how_to_validate.rationale: MedQA validation does not transfer to SWE benchmarks.

candidates.match_note: Off-topic for software engineering claim.

candidates.paper_title: Accurate medium-range global weather forecasting with 3D neural networks

useful_outcomes.rationale: Pangu-Weather forecasting — not software engineering.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Weather-prediction model, not a code agent.

plausible.rationale: Excellent methodology in its own field.

how_to_validate.rationale: ECMWF-benchmark validation does not transfer to SWE.

candidates.match_note: Off-topic for the SWE claim.

candidates.paper_title: Logical quantum processor based on reconfigurable atom arrays

useful_outcomes.rationale: Quantum-computing hardware result; not software engineering.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Not adoptable by SWE-agent practitioners.

plausible.rationale: Excellent physics methodology.

how_to_validate.rationale: QEC validation does not transfer.

candidates.match_note: Off-topic for SWE.

candidates.paper_title: Autonomous chemical research with large language models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Coscientist's autonomous lab agent uses code execution; tangential to SWE.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Architecture useful inspiration; not directly for SWE.

plausible.rationale: Validated on real chemistry experiments.

how_to_validate.rationale: Wet-lab validation does not transfer to SWE benchmarks.

candidates.match_note: Scientific-research agent rather than SWE.

candidates.paper_title: Visual Instruction Tuning

useful_outcomes.rationale: LLaVA — multimodal model, not a SWE benchmark contribution.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Some code-screenshot use cases but not core SWE-agent stack.

plausible.rationale: Foundational multimodal methodology.

how_to_validate.rationale: MM-Vet/Science-QA validation does not transfer to SWE.

candidates.match_note: Vision-language foundation; off-topic for SWE claim.

candidates.paper_title: Toward expert-level medical question answering with large language models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Med-PaLM 2 medical QA — off-topic for SWE.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Not adoptable for code agents.

plausible.rationale: Strong medical-domain methodology.

how_to_validate.rationale: Clinical validation does not transfer to SWE.

candidates.match_note: Off-topic for SWE.

candidates.paper_title: A whole-slide foundation model for digital pathology from real-world data

useful_outcomes.rationale: Prov-GigaPath pathology foundation model — off-topic for SWE.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Used by pathologists, not SWE-agent builders.

plausible.rationale: Excellent pathology methodology.

how_to_validate.rationale: Pathology validation does not transfer.

candidates.match_note: Off-topic for SWE.

applications-web-and-embodied.claim_text: Web and GUI agents (WebArena, Mind2Web, computer-use models) and embodied LLM agents (PaLM-E, RT-2, VLA models) translate textual reasoning into clicks, keystrokes, and physical actions — opening up the action space agents can operate in.

candidates.paper_title: VisualWebArena: Evaluating Multimodal Agents on Realistic Visual Web Tasks

useful_outcomes.rationale: Defines VisualWebArena, a realistic visual-web benchmark with 910 tasks; best agent (GPT-4V SoM) hits ~16% success — quantifies the gap directly.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Reproducible benchmark immediately adoptable by web-agent practitioners.

plausible.rationale: Held-out tasks across Classifieds/Shopping/Reddit-like sites; explicit visual grounding.

scalable.rationale: Benchmark harness scales to fleets of agents; current models still far from solved.

how_to_validate.rationale: Task-success-rate validation is fully transferable.

candidates.match_note: Canonical visual-web-agent benchmark; squarely on-claim.

candidates.paper_title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control

useful_outcomes.rationale: Co-fine-tuning a VLM on robot trajectories yields large emergent-reasoning gains on unseen-object manipulation tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: VLA recipe is directly adoptable on RT-1-like robot stacks.

plausible.rationale: End-to-end training and broad real-robot evaluation are well-described.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on production-scale robot fleets at Google.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out manipulation suites and emergent-skill probes provide transferable validation.

candidates.match_note: Flagship VLA paper directly named in claim text.

candidates.paper_title: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model

useful_outcomes.rationale: 562B PaLM-E integrates vision/state into LM; strong embodied-task and OK-VQA results, and positive transfer across robot embodiments.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Architecture pattern adopted across VLAs; the model itself is closed.

plausible.rationale: Multi-embodiment training is rigorously evaluated.

how_to_validate.rationale: Robot success-rate and VQA validation transfer to follow-on embodied agents.

candidates.match_note: Named in the claim; canonical embodied LLM.

candidates.paper_title: OpenVLA: An Open-Source Vision-Language-Action Model

useful_outcomes.rationale: 7B open VLA matches/beats RT-2-X (55B) on 29 manipulation tasks across embodiments while being 7x smaller.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Weights and fine-tuning recipes are open; usable today on a single GPU with LoRA.

plausible.rationale: Trained on Open X-Embodiment; ablations cover LoRA fine-tuning and quantization.

scalable.rationale: Efficient fine-tuning scales VLA deployment to many labs.

how_to_validate.rationale: Bridge V2 and other embodied benchmarks provide transferable validation.

candidates.match_note: Open-source flagship VLA; squarely on-claim.

candidates.paper_title: RT-1: Robotics Transformer for Real-World Control at Scale

useful_outcomes.rationale: RT-1 trained on 130k episodes / 700 tasks achieves 97% success on training tasks and strong generalization to unseen tasks/environments.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Recipe and dataset are public; widely reused as VLA precursor.

plausible.rationale: Large-scale real-robot data collection rigorously evaluated.

how_to_validate.rationale: Real-robot held-out tasks transfer.

candidates.match_note: Direct embodied-agent contribution; foundational for VLA line in claim.

candidates.paper_title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: First LLM-powered lifelong agent in Minecraft; obtains 3.3x more unique items, traverses 2.3x longer distances, and unlocks key tech-tree milestones 15.3x faster than baselines.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Skill-library and curriculum patterns are reusable; Minecraft sandbox is open.

plausible.rationale: Sound methodology with executable skill memory and self-verification.

scalable.rationale: Game-environment scale; concept extends to broader embodied domains.

how_to_validate.rationale: Minecraft tech-tree milestones provide a reproducible validation harness.

candidates.match_note: Embodied open-ended agent in a virtual environment; slight gap from physical/web action claim.

candidates.paper_title: Visual Instruction Tuning

useful_outcomes.rationale: LLaVA establishes the multimodal-instruction backbone underpinning many web-GUI agents (e.g. SoM, screenshot understanding).

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open weights heavily reused in web/GUI-agent stacks.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous instruction-tuning methodology.

how_to_validate.rationale: ScienceQA/MM-Vet validation transfers to VQA but not directly to web-task success.

candidates.match_note: Multimodal foundation enabling web-GUI agents; not itself a web/embodied agent.

candidates.paper_title: Augmenting large language models with chemistry tools

useful_outcomes.rationale: ChemCrow plans/executes synthesis with 18 tools — a scientific-tool agent, not web/embodied.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Limited adoptability for web/GUI/embodied practitioners.

plausible.rationale: Validated against expert chemists.

how_to_validate.rationale: Chemistry validation does not transfer.

candidates.match_note: Scientific tool-using agent, not web/embodied.

candidates.paper_title: A Multitask, Multilingual, Multimodal Evaluation of ChatGPT on Reasoning, Hallucination, and Interactivity

useful_outcomes.rationale: Evaluates ChatGPT on 23 datasets; tangential to web/embodied action.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Findings inform agent designers but no agent is released.

plausible.rationale: Sound evaluation methodology.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation does not transfer to web/embodied success-rate metrics.

candidates.match_note: General ChatGPT eval; tangential to web/embodied claim.

applications-scientific-research.claim_text: Research co-pilot agents for biomedicine, chemistry, and materials (ChemCrow, Coscientist, Biomni, GeneAgent, agentic rare-disease diagnosis) demonstrate that agents can plan and execute multi-step scientific workflows — including hypothesis generation, literature synthesis, and wet-lab planning.

candidates.paper_title: Autonomous chemical research with large language models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Coscientist autonomously designs, plans, and executes complex chemistry experiments (e.g. Pd-catalysed cross-couplings) on real lab hardware — directly named in the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Architecture (web/doc search + code-execution + lab automation) is directly reusable as a scientific-agent template.

plausible.rationale: Nature-grade methodology with reproducible task suite.

scalable.rationale: Single-lab scale but generalises to other automated platforms.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation via actual reaction-yield outcomes on six task families is transferable.

candidates.match_note: Coscientist is one of the named exemplars in the claim.

candidates.paper_title: Large language models encode clinical knowledge

useful_outcomes.rationale: Med-PaLM achieves 67.6% on MedQA, +17% over prior SOTA — a major biomedical research-copilot building block.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Instruction-prompt-tuning recipe is reusable for domain-specific biomedical agents.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous clinician-graded human evaluation.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out medical QA + clinician panels transfer to other biomedical assistants.

candidates.match_note: Biomedical foundation model, not agentic, but supports research-copilot claim.

candidates.paper_title: Toward expert-level medical question answering with large language models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Med-PaLM 2 reaches 86.5% on MedQA with ensemble refinement and chain-of-retrieval — directly relevant to biomedical research agents.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Ensemble-refinement and chain-of-retrieval techniques are reusable in scientific-research agents.

plausible.rationale: Multi-stage clinician evaluation.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out MedQA + long-form physician evaluations transfer.

candidates.match_note: Strong medical-QA pillar for biomedical research copilots.

candidates.paper_title: Adapted large language models can outperform medical experts in clinical text summarization

useful_outcomes.rationale: Adapted LLMs outperform clinician baselines on multiple summarization tasks — a literature-synthesis primitive named in the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Fine-tuning recipes directly adoptable by biomedical research-agent builders.

plausible.rationale: Multi-task evaluation with clinician scoring.

how_to_validate.rationale: Clinician-graded held-out summarization transfers.

candidates.match_note: Literature-synthesis component of research-copilot vision.

candidates.paper_title: Foundation models for generalist medical artificial intelligence

useful_outcomes.rationale: Proposes GMAI as flexible foundation models across imaging/EHR/genomics — articulates the research-copilot vision.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Conceptual roadmap rather than a deployable system.

plausible.rationale: Nature perspective grounded in empirical foundation-model results.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation strategies discussed but not benchmarked.

candidates.match_note: Vision paper supporting biomedical-agent direction.

candidates.paper_title: Segment anything in medical images

useful_outcomes.rationale: MedSAM trained on 1.57M image-mask pairs covering 10 modalities and 30+ cancer types — a universal segmentation tool for biomedical agents.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open model immediately usable as a tool inside biomedical research pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Large-scale, multi-modality evaluation.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out modality/disease benchmarks transfer.

candidates.match_note: Foundation tool for biomedical-research agents.

candidates.paper_title: Scaling deep learning for materials discovery

useful_outcomes.rationale: GNoME discovers 2.2M new inorganic crystals (381k stable) — direct materials-research outcome.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Database release is immediately useful to materials-discovery agents.

plausible.rationale: Nature-grade methodology with experimental synthesis follow-up.

how_to_validate.rationale: DFT and experimental synthesis validation transfer.

candidates.match_note: Materials-discovery component of the claim.

candidates.paper_title: Transfer learning enables predictions in network biology

useful_outcomes.rationale: Geneformer enables transfer-learning predictions on single-cell network biology — supports GeneAgent-style scientific agents.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open model and pretraining corpus directly reusable in biomedical pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Validated across multiple cell types and tasks.

scalable.rationale: Scales with corpus and cell-type coverage.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out cell types and biological-task transfer benchmarks.

candidates.match_note: Biology foundation model underpinning research agents.

candidates.paper_title: A foundation model for generalizable disease detection from retinal images

useful_outcomes.rationale: RETFound retinal foundation model generalises across multiple eye-disease tasks — biomedical research-tool outcome.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open weights directly adoptable as a tool in research pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Validated on diverse held-out clinical datasets.

how_to_validate.rationale: Held-out disease-detection benchmarks transfer.

candidates.match_note: Biomedical foundation tool for research agents.

candidates.paper_title: Sparks of Artificial General Intelligence: Early experiments with GPT-4

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reports broad emergent reasoning across math/coding/medicine that motivates scientific-copilot use.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Findings inform which tasks scientific agents should target; no system released.

plausible.rationale: Largely qualitative case studies; rigorous but not benchmark-driven.

how_to_validate.rationale: Case-study evaluation transfers loosely.

candidates.match_note: Capability survey rather than a scientific-research agent.
